teamObjective.explanation: I have only two cards left and the lead. My priority is to play both cards and go out before opponents, securing a first-place finish for my team.

partnerBelief.summary: Partner has 3 cards and no public information about their strength.
evidence: Hand count shows partner (player 2) holds 3 cards.
No cards have been played, so partner's distribution is unknown.

opponentBelief.summary: Opponents each hold 3 cards; they likely have playable answers to a low single but may not beat an Ace.
evidence: Hand counts show opponents (players 1 and 3) each hold 3 cards.
No trump cards or bombs have been played.

actionRationale.primaryReason: Leading the weaker 2 of spades preserves the Ace as a winning card for my next lead. If opponents pass on the 2, I regain the lead and can play the Ace to go out.

whyNotAlternatives.reason: Leading the Ace risks an opponent playing a trump or bomb, leaving me stuck with the weak 2 and losing control of the endgame.

risks: An opponent may play a higher single on the 2 and take the lead.
An opponent may have a bomb or trump to interrupt my plan.
riskAssessment.mitigation: By leading low first, I keep my strongest remaining card to try to win back the lead if necessary.
